perturbation.original_text: The CDQ and CDQ power generation systems shall comply with the national environmental protection standards. If the environment is polluted during the operation of the power plant, Party A shall bear the liability.
perturbation.changed_text: If the environment is polluted during the operation of the power plant, Party A shall bear the liability.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement that the CDQ and CDQ power generation systems must comply with national environmental protection standards, the contract introduces a significant legal risk. This omission contradicts environmental laws that mandate specific compliance measures for such facilities.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Environmental Protection Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 18 of the Environmental Protection Law of the People's Republic of China stipulates that enterprises and public institutions must comply with national environmental protection standards. Removing this requirement from the contract allows the power generation systems to potentially operate without adhering to these legally mandated standards, creating a direct contradiction.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates that the CDQ and CDQ power generation systems comply with national environmental protection standards, while the changed text omits this requirement. The Perturbation Explanation indicates this omission contradicts environmental laws. The Law Explanation supports this, stating that enterprises and public institutions must comply with national environmental protection standards, and removing this requirement creates a direct contradiction. Since the omission removes a legally mandated standard for environmental compliance, it creates a legal contradiction under the Omission Legal Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: Party A is responsible for the equipment quality, technical performance, and construction quality. Party B is responsible for the technical specifications and energy media quality.
perturbation.changed_text: Party B is responsible for the technical specifications and energy media quality.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the statement that 'Party A is responsible for the equipment quality, technical performance, and construction quality,' the contract introduces a significant loophole regarding product safety and liability.  This omission potentially contradicts consumer protection laws regarding product liability if the equipment is faulty and causes harm.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Product Quality Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 26 of the Product Quality Law of the People's Republic of China states that producers are responsible for the quality of their products and must ensure they meet safety standards. By omitting Party A's responsibility for equipment quality, the contract weakens consumer protection and potentially violates this law if the equipment is defective.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-606. What Constitutes Acceptance of Goods. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-606. What Constitutes Acceptance of Goods. (1) Acceptance of goods occurs when the buyer (a) after a reasonable opportunity to inspect the goods signifies to the seller that the goods are conforming or that he will take or retain them in spite of their non-conformity; or (b) fails to make an effective rejection (subsection (1) of Section 2-602 ), but such acceptance does not occur until the buyer has had a reasonable opportunity to inspect them; or (c) does any act inconsistent with the seller's ownership; but if such act is wrongful as against the seller it is an acceptance only if ratified by him. (2) Acceptance of a part of any commercial unit is acceptance of that entire unit. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The omission of Party A's responsibility for equipment quality directly contradicts the principle that producers are responsible for product quality and safety. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 discusses acceptance of goods under the Uniform Commercial Code, it indirectly supports the idea that goods should conform to standards and buyers have the right to inspect them. The original contract ensures shared responsibility, while the modified version creates a loophole, potentially violating consumer protection laws, as explained in the Law Explanation, by removing Party A's accountability for defective equipment.

perturbation.original_text: The intellectual property of the project belongs to Party A. Without written consent of Party A, Party B is not allowed to disclose the intellectual property to the other third party.
perturbation.changed_text: Without written consent of Party A, Party B is not allowed to disclose the intellectual property to the other third party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the statement that 'The intellectual property of the project belongs to Party A', it creates ambiguity around the ownership of intellectual property. This omission contradicts intellectual property laws that specify how ownership should be determined and protected.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Copyright Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 11 of the Copyright Law specifies how the ownership of copyright should be determined. By omitting the clear statement of ownership, it is opening the contract up to possible dispute in the future, as it is not defined who owns the technology and has the right to protection for such technology.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: ement, and counter statements and also any other documents that may be filed by either party to the suit. Hearings - The Draft Rules also delineate provisions pertaining to the hearing of patent suits and also about first, second, and third case management hearing. These stages of hearings enumerate the course of action which the Court may take while hearing patent suits. Constitution of Confidentiality club - The Rules provide for the constitution of confidentiality club by the Court for the preservation of confidential information between the parties. Compulsory mediation - The Court may at any stage of the suit appoint a mediator if it is of the opinion that the parties to the suit shall explore settlement through mediation Scientific Advisors Panel - shall be drawn up by The Delhi High Court to assist judges while deciding patent suits. Hot–tubbing - The Rules propose a hot-tubbing method- a modern method adopted to record evidence during the Second Case Management Hearing. Audio/Video recording of evidence has been permitted. Special permission to allow video recording of evidence. Independent Technical Experts could be chosen from each side and be directed to be present for the final hearing to assist the Court. The Delhi High Court has formulated the Draft Rules in a manner that it helps in eliminating the various complexities that are faced by the Hon'ble Court while adjudicating patent suits. The Rules briefly provide for all the necessary elements i.e. the content and documents that are to be included in plaints, written statements, and counterclaims. The stage of hearing has also been bifurcated and the suggested course of action for each stage of hearing has been illustrated. If the Rules are followed by parties to patent suits in true letter and spirit, then it is expected that the same may result in the timely disposal of patent infringement cases in India. perturbation.justification: The original contract text explicitly states 'The intellectual property of the project belongs to Party A', while the changed text omits this statement. This omission contradicts intellectual property laws because it creates ambiguity regarding ownership, potentially leading to future disputes. The Law Explanation supports this by stating the omission opens the contract to possible dispute, as it's undefined who owns the technology. While the provided snippets do not directly address copyright law they imply the need for clearly defined legal agreements to avoid disputes. The explanation of omissions requiring a 'legally mandated consumer protection' also applies.
